sections.question_synthetic: What sponsorship identification requirements apply to the California Coordinating Council (CCC) as a sponsored committee with multiple sponsors from different industries, and must CCC include sponsor names in its committee name?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: CCC cannot utilize the multiple sponsor exception to Regulation 18419(b)(1) because no single term describes its multiple sponsors from different groups and industries. Therefore, CCC may be subject to full sponsorship identification requirements under Section 84106, potentially requiring disclosure of all sponsors' names, or at minimum more than one identifying term describing the different sponsor groups.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a formal written confirmation of prior telephone advice. No separate QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers; extracted from narrative letter format. References prior advice letter A-88-284 (Olson). Dated August 21, 1990.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What sponsorship identification requirements apply to the California Coordinating Council (CCC) as a sponsored committee with multiple sponsors from different industries, and must CCC include sponsor names in its committee name?

CONCLUSION: CCC cannot utilize the multiple sponsor exception to Regulation 18419(b)(1) because no single term describes its multiple sponsors from different groups and industries. Therefore, CCC may be subject to full sponsorship identification requirements under Section 84106, potentially requiring disclosure of all sponsors' names, or at minimum more than one identifying term describing the different sponsor groups.

embedding.summary: FPPC clarifies prior telephone advice regarding sponsorship identification requirements for the California Coordinating Council. The letter confirms CCC is a sponsored committee but corrects the implication that it is exempt from sponsorship identification requirements, explaining instead that it may require disclosure of all sponsors or multiple identifying terms.
